MX: Volcanic highlands cross the interior; karst sinks on humid tropical limestone flats
MY: Rainforest on granite uplifts; mangrove tidal straits on narrow continental shelves
MZ: Coastal plain behind barrier islands; rift escarpments west to miombo uplands
NA: Fog-fed gravel plains along cold surf; inland canyons in layered basement quartzites
NE: Sandstone massifs from vast gravel plains; dark inselbergs between winding wadis
NG: Braided mangrove delta on a humid shore; dissected plateaus between savanna belts inland
NI: Volcanic cones over lake floors; Pacific lowlands west of a pine-forest high spine
NL: Polder grids below sea level behind dikes; peat mires on reclaimed estuary muds
NO: Glacial fjords in old gneiss; avalanche chutes on alpine walls above strandflats
NP: Icewalls above monsoon forest; terraces down to steamy alluvial piedmont fans
NR: Limestone plateau with terrace cliffs; phosphate pinnacles over barren inland karst
NZ: Volcanic plateau with crater lakes; southern icefields feeding braided graywacke plains
OM: Summer drizzle cloud forest on south slopes; arid limestone wadis inland on gravel hammada
PA: Rainforest isthmus between two oceans; volcanic spine with cloud ridges and gap winds
PE: Arid coastal desert with offshore fog; ice-capped cordillera above high interior basins
PG: Humid cordilleran ridges; braided fans at mountain fronts above swampy piedmont
PH: Steep volcanic arcs above deep trenches; typhoon surf on east-facing reef margins
PK: Braided Indus channels on desert flats; fold ranges west with gravel fans at fronts
PL: Terminal moraine forests; myriad kettle lakes on glacial outwash sheets northward
PR: Limestone karst towers under trade-wind rain; cloud-forest crest above narrow shelf seas
PS: Limestone hills to rift scarps; wadis on arid marl stepping toward interior basin floors
PT: Granite Atlantic headlands; rolling schist hills under evergreen oak savanna inland
PW: Limestone islets; marine lakes between double reef passes opening to deep swells
PY: Mega-river alluvial swamps; thorn scrub on windblown Pleistocene sand sheets inland
QA: Low limestone peninsula with sabkhas; shallow Gulf shallows and tidal channel mazes
